mechanism_narrative: A surprise monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals an unexpected weakening of the labor market, causing a rapid negative repricing of short-term growth expectations by investors.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is a timely, low-revision indicator of labor market health watched closely on its release day.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of instances immediately following a monthly UNRATE increase of more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate provides a persistent tailwind for equities by lowering the discount rate on future earnings and encouraging risk-taking via the portfolio balance channel, as other safe assets offer negligible returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.08% in the June 2020 vintage, indicating maximum monetary accommodation.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS (when below 1%) and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in retail sales signals that consumer demand is rebounding strongly, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Massive fiscal stimulus (CARES Act) was deployed in Q2 2020 to support consumption.
failure_condition: Instances of rapid RSAFS acceleration are not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time during post-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent positive momentum in both industrial production and payroll employment indicates a broad-based, self-sustaining economic recovery. This joint signal is stronger than either indicator alone, boosting investor confidence and equity prices.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO > 0 and 3-month change in PAYEMS > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Both series showed sharp rebounds in May/June 2020 after the April trough.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction term of INDPRO and PAYEMS momentum does not show a statistically significant positive coefficient (p > 0.05) for 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation beyond the central bank's target raises expectations of future monetary tightening. This increases the discount rate for equities and signals potential for lower corporate margins, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: doc:Federal Reserve's dual mandate includes price stability.
failure_condition: Periods where YoY CPIAUCSL is above 2.5% do not exhibit statistically lower 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to periods when it is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for the broader economy due to its high multiplier effect on construction, materials, and durable goods consumption, thus predicting positive medium-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Low mortgage rates in mid-2020 were expected to support the housing market.
failure_condition: The 6-month rolling change in HOUST has a Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market becomes extremely tight, as indicated by a very low unemployment rate, it signals rising wage pressures that compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Pre-COVID (2019) unemployment was below 4%, a condition associated with late-cycle concerns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are not statistically lower (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when UNRATE is below 4.0% compared to when it is between 4.0% and 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth is positive but retail sales growth is negative indicates an unhealthy economic composition where consumption, the primary driver of the US economy, is faltering. This interaction predicts negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter % change in GDPC1 > 0 and Quarter-over-quarter % change in RSAFS < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: A strategy that shorts the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha over a full business cycle.
